title: SecID Conformance Test Fixture Suite
description: JSON Schema for the SecID resolver conformance test fixture format. A fixture suite is a list of test cases; each case describes an HTTP request to issue against a resolver and the expected response shape. Behavioral assertions are the minimum contract; strict assertions optionally pin byte-identical output.

suite_version.description: Suite format version. Major version changes signal breaking format changes; minor for additions.

description.description: Free-text description of what this suite covers.

name.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for this test case. Used as the test ID by the harness.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this test verifies.

category.description: Hierarchical category for filtering and reporting (e.g., 'resolver/advisory/cve').

variants.description: Named variants of this test. Most common: 'with_data' for verbose-mode tests. Variants inherit input from the parent test and override specific fields.

endpoint.description: Path under the target resolver's base URL (e.g., '/api/v1/resolve').

behavioral.description: Behavioral assertions — the minimum contract every conforming resolver must satisfy. These are the hard failures.

response_status.description: Expected value of the top-level 'status' field in the response (e.g., 'found', 'not_found', 'corrected').

max_results.description: Maximum number of results expected. Omit for unbounded.

must_have_paths.description: JSON-pointer-like paths that MUST be present in the response. Each path is an array of keys/indices.

must_not_have_paths.description: Paths that MUST NOT be present in the response. Useful for asserting absence (e.g., no 'data' field by default).

field_assertions.description: Per-field assertions, keyed by JSON-pointer-like path strings (e.g., 'results[0].url').

fieldAssertion.description: An assertion on a specific field's value. Exactly one of regex/equals/contains/in must be present.

regex.description: ECMA regex the value must match.

equals.description: Exact JSON value the field must equal.

contains.description: Substring the value must contain (string fields only).

in.description: Set of acceptable values. Field must equal one of them.

strict.description: Strict assertions — full canonical response shape. Only run when the harness is invoked with --strict.

body.description: Full expected response body. Compared with key-order-insensitive deep equality.

variant.description: A named variation of the parent test, e.g., 'with_data' to test the ?include_data=true mode. Inherits the parent's input and expected, with the fields below overriding.

input_override.description: Fields to merge into the parent's input. For query overrides, the merged value wins.
